gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/370)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/370)
text: مَنْ قَالَهَا سَبْعَ مَرَّاتٍ رَدَّ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ كُلِّ عَبْدٍ حَسَنَةً وَ كَانَ عَمَلُهُ فِي ذَلِكَ الْيَوْمِ مَقْبُولًا وَ جَاءَ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ وَ بَيْنَ عَيْنَيْهِ نُورٌ .

en.hubeali: ‘(He<sup>asws</sup> said): ‘The one who says it seven times, Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Revert one good deed upon him from every servant, and his deeds during that day would be Acceptable, and he would come on the Day of Judgement, and in front of him would be a Light’.
en.sarwar: 5. The narrator of the previous Hadith has narrated that if one says the expression stated in the previous Hadith seven times, Allah in return grants him one good from every servant. On that day his deeds are accepted and on the Day of Judgment he will come with a light between his eyes.”
